headline: Barcelona's Sagrada Familia Finally Complete, Honored in LEGO
meta_description: After 142 years of construction, Barcelona's Sagrada Familia basilica is finally complete—and LEGO is marking the milestone with its largest architecture set e…
summary_short: The iconic Sagrada Familia basilica in Barcelona is now complete after 142 years. LEGO celebrates with a 12,060-piece set releasing this November.
body:
When Antoni Gaudí took over the Sagrada Familia project in 1882, he envisioned 18 towers rising higher than any church in Europe. What he couldn't have imagined: that 142 years later, his masterpiece would be honored not just in Barcelona, but in plastic bricks on living room tables worldwide.

This November marks two milestones. Barcelona's most famous basilica opens to the public on November 10th, finally complete after more than a century of construction that survived wars, lost blueprints, and even decades of building without official permission. That same month, LEGO releases its largest architecture series set ever: a faithful 24-inch tall replica containing 12,060 pieces, priced at $800.

The timing is deliberate. This year marks the 100th anniversary of Gaudí's death in 1926, when his original plans vanished and builders had to continue from memory and instinct alone. The LEGO designers faced a similar creative challenge—translating the basilica's extraordinary complexity into an immersive building experience.

What makes the set special is its attention to Gaudí's vision. Transparent bricks allow light to filter through, just as it does in the real basilica. Every architectural detail captures the rhythm of construction that has captivated Barcelona for generations.

Sometimes the most ambitious dreams take longer than expected. Now, they're finally within reach.
